text.title: Notion Views and API Rules
text.summary: View configuration rules, Notion REST API patterns for agent task management, and automation integration.

rule.statement: Create purpose-specific views: Board (sprint execution, grouped by Status, filtered to current sprint), Table (all tasks, sortable), Timeline (roadmap, grouped by Project, date field = Date Range), Calendar (deadlines, filtered to not-Done). Never create a catch-all view showing everything — it loads slowly and has no clear use case.
rule.rationale: Each view should answer one specific question. Views that show all data for all purposes become the default view and reduce the value of purpose-specific filters.

rule.statement: Use linked databases (not duplicated databases) to show the same data in multiple pages. Linked databases point to the same source — changes propagate automatically. Duplicating databases creates two separate sources of truth.
rule.rationale: Duplicated databases require manual sync. Linked databases always reflect the source state without manual updates.

rule.statement: Use database_id (UUID format) for all API operations. Property names in API calls must match exactly the display names in the Notion UI — including case and spacing. Store database_ids in .aidocs/memory/ or constitution.md.
rule.rationale: Property name mismatches cause silent failures (the API returns 200 but does not update the property). Case sensitivity is a frequent source of agent errors.

rule.statement: Use databases.query endpoint with filter and sort parameters rather than pages.retrieve for finding tasks. The query endpoint supports AND/OR filter trees and pagination — pages.retrieve only works when you already know the page_id.
rule.rationale: Agents that scan all database pages via pages.retrieve are making N API calls for N tasks. databases.query returns all matching items in one call with server-side filtering.

rule.statement: Use Notion's native automations for simple property-change triggers (set Completion Date when Status = Done, notify on Urgent priority). Use Zapier or Make (n8n) for cross-tool workflows (Notion task created → Slack notification, GitHub PR merged → Notion task status update).
rule.rationale: Notion native automations run within Notion and are simpler to configure. Cross-tool workflows require an external automation platform because Notion cannot call external APIs natively in automation actions.

text.reference: Sprint page template with metrics and retrospective
